mood.label: RISK-ON
mood.summary: Geopolitical tensions are easing as a potential US-Iran deal appears close, fueling a strong rebound in equities and a rotation back into tech and growth.

indicesNarrative: U.S. markets staged a powerful rebound on Thursday, driven by signs of a potential peace deal between the U.S. and Iran, which sent oil prices tumbling. Tech and small-caps led the charge, with the Nasdaq soaring 2.54% and the Russell 2000 jumping 3%. Pre-market futures are pointing to a higher open, continuing the positive momentum as Asian markets rallied strongly overnight.
technicalsNarrative: Technical data was not available at the time of generation. Cannot assess entry risk or momentum.

sectorsThemes.name: Technology
sectorsThemes.note: Led the market rebound, with semiconductors showing significant strength.

sectorsThemes.name: Semiconductors
sectorsThemes.note: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index surged as all 30 components ended higher.

sectorsThemes.name: Energy
sectorsThemes.note: Underperformed as oil prices dropped on hopes of a U.S.-Iran deal.

sectorsThemes.name: Small Caps
sectorsThemes.note: Showed strong risk appetite with a 3% gain, outperforming large caps.

sectorsNarrative: A clear risk-on rotation was visible, with technology and small-caps leading the market higher. Energy stocks lagged significantly due to the sharp drop in crude oil prices.
industryNarrative: Semiconductors were the standout industry group, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index surging nearly 8%. This indicates a strong return of speculative appetite. Conversely, oil and gas related industries faced headwinds.

movers.why: Dropped despite a Q4 earnings beat, as the CFO guided for lower gross margins in FY27, raising concerns about future profitability.
Advanced in pre-market trading, leading a rebound in chip stocks.
Climbed along with other semiconductor equipment makers amid renewed sector strength.

traderLens.name: Minervini
traderLens.view: Market showing signs of a potential Stage 2 continuation after a pullback. Focus on leaders emerging from the recent volatility.

traderLens.name: Ted Zhang
traderLens.view: Strong rotation back into Technology and Semiconductors. This is where the institutional flow is heading.

traderLens.name: Clement Ang
traderLens.view: Market context has improved significantly with the geopolitical de-escalation. A-grade setups are now in play.

traderLens.name: SRxTrades
traderLens.view: Look for volume confirmation on today's follow-through. Avoid chasing extended names; wait for clean pivots.

traderLens.name: Jeff
traderLens.view: The sharp rebound is a reminder to stick to the plan. Don't get chopped up by headline volatility; respect stops.

traderLens.name: Qullamaggie
traderLens.view: The powerful up-move creates potential for breakout setups. Look for stocks clearing key resistance levels on high volume.

traderLens.name: Composite
traderLens.view: The tape has turned bullish on the back of easing geopolitical fears. Focus on leading tech and semiconductor names, but be mindful of potential volatility. Posture is GO, but with disciplined entries.

alert: Geopolitical tensions ease as US-Iran deal appears imminent; strong risk-on sentiment returns.

news.headline: U.S. stock futures rise as Trump touts Iran peace deal
Asian stocks rally on easing US-Iran tensions and upcoming SpaceX debut
Oil prices plunge as threat of military action against Iran recedes
